channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, May 15, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Navigational tool
  • Hint 2: Uses magnetized needles
  • Hint 3: Helps you find North

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Sextant
  • Accelerometer
  • Protractor
  • Nautical
  • Navigational
  • Barometer
  • Navigation
  • Altimeter
  • GPS

Today’s Contexto Answer For May 15, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Compass

A Compass is a navigational instrument used to determine direction, typically featuring a megnetized needle that aligns with the Earth’s magnetic North.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 16.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: CoJo Denies Milan Benching was a “Panic decision” after Social Media Scrutiny
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic Valorant Team Director Colin ‘CoJo’ Johnson has further explained Fnatic’s decision to drop Milan ‘Milan’ de Meij as the org’s Valorant head coach.

The Fnatic Valorant head responded to “questions” on social media surrounding the decision to let go of the coach who had led Fnatic to EMEA Stage 1 victory, and three international Grand Finals in 2025, dismissing the idea that it was a “panic decision” to remove the coach.

Analysts and Casters question the timing of Milan decision

CoJo was not simply replying to phantoms. Canadian caster, Shayan ‘ShyoWager’ Shehrya, and VCT EMEA’s Tom ‘Tombizz’ Bissmire were amongst those to question the timing of the decision, with Tombizz stating that it “feels like a rash decision.”

Particular subjects of focus were Fnatic’s unfortunate circumstances after Sylvain ‘Veqaj’ Pattyn’s illness interrupted Fnatic’s season, in which they boasted a flawless 5-0 Group Stage run.

Tombizz stated, “Considering the team went 5-0 and then lost to Vitality and Heretics (only just) with a stand-in this feels like a rash decision. Milan got promoted last year, and the team went from bombing out of Kick Off to multiple 2nd places. Hope to see him land on his feet soon…”

“Don’t necessarily think a season of subs and league match domination, in combination with facing leveled up teams in playoffs warrants a change,” added ShyoWager.

The notable Fortnite caster added, “I think the meta would be going towards the Milan ideology as well coming into Stage 2. They just had a few mistakes defensively to fix. Would have loved to see his vision make it to an international this year on FNC.”

That first point was an opinion shared by many community members in reply to the initial announcment. Questions hung over Milan’s adaptability to the 11.08 changes which made the game significantly faster, more Duelist-focussed, and more aggressive.

However, that meta is soon to end as the 13.00 patch promises Initiator and Sentinel buffs, aiming to rebalance the importance of teamplay with individual explosivity.

The slowing down of the game was expected to benefit Fnatic’s playstyle, with Milan and Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett seemingly prioritizing map control, teamplay, and optimal decision-making as core values.

CoJo clarifies decision-making process for Milan benching

Following online discource, CoJo clarified the decision-making process within Fnatic for Milan’s removal, stating that “Decisions are never made without me discussing with every player,” adding, “Roster/staff decisions are almost always made with a minimum of 2-3 review cycles (3 months per cycle normally) worth of feedback.”

The Fnatic Valorant lead rebuffed the concept that the decision resulted from Fnatic’s unfortunate circumstances which saw them miss out on Valorant Masters London qualification.

CoJo stated, “I’m not a dictator or making decisions because we lost a 12-9 and had stand-ins. This Milan decision alone I had 20 hours at least of just me talking with people in the team before anything was decided.”

Finally, reiterating that the decision was not made rashly, stating, “Feel free to blame me for s**t if you want, I don’t mind, but just don’t think this was a panic decision.”

That added context shows that there are more problems within the team that the players feel need to be addressed beyond the roster issues they have faced in recent weeks, as Fnatic now face an uphill battle to qualify for Valorant Champions 2026 in Shanghai.

The roster missed their second international of the year for the first time after their VCT EMEA Stage 1 elimination. All efforts will be to ensure they do not miss their third.

Is it the right decision? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.
For more VCT news, Cloud9 has announced Xeppaa is taking a break from competitive Valorant.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/cojo-clarifies-fnatic-milan-decision/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon is Getting Two New PvE Modes in Season Two
item.content:encoded:
Bungie has revealed that Marathon is slated to get two new, experimental PvE modes going into season two, which is named Nightfall. It lands on June 2, expanding the game’s content base and adding new ways to play, but the team at Bungie is using the opportunity to flesh out more of the core game.

With that being said, they’re looking at ‘fun and mind-bending content’, new and updated zones, fresh Runner shells, and PvE-focused modes. This will arguably open up a new audience for Marathon, which has been struggling with player retention of late.

Marathon is Going PvE

Around Marathon’s launch, Bungie stressed that it didn’t want a PvE-only mode because it takes away from the unpredictability and inherent volatility of a PvP-focused game. As this was always designed to be a ‘social’ extraction shooter, they always wanted to maintain that player presence.

However, with player counts dropping and many in the extraction world desiring a PvE experience, Bungie has opted to do just that.

In season two, Bungie will focus more on PvE contributions, adding two modes, one at the opening of the season and another in the latter half.

The first will be an experimental mode that will be considered a ‘PvE-lite’ offering, focusing on player-vs-environment combat while still offering a light touch of PvP. In the latter half of the season, a pure PvE mode will drop, allowing players to complete objectives together and progress across matches as a unit.

The team explained:

We’ll continue to run experimental queues beyond these two offerings for Season 2 and beyond, testing the waters around other mechanics (such as perhaps a more purely PVP-focused mode) with the potential for these to become part of the core game loop or permanent new ways to play.

It was then promised that season three will bring a huge revision of the early-game experience, adjusting what was available at launch based on the stacks of feedback received since the game came out.

With that being said, the next few seasons were outlined:

  • Season two is about future content plans and ‘sowing the seeds’ of what comes next with Sentinel and Night Marsh
  • Season three is based on revision and updating what exists, and adding a new Runner shell
  • Season four will be about building more depth into the game’s extraction loop
  • And season five will be focused on bringing the PvPvE extraction world together to ‘evolve the weird sci-fi world in new ways’

So, plenty is coming, but whether the wider audience will be there to actively receive it is another question entirely.

Are you still playing Marathon? Let us know your thoughts about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that COD Mobile is getting some massive updates

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Los Angeles Chargers Use Halo To Announce Their Schedule
item.content:encoded:
The Los Angeles Chargers have added a video game flair to their promotional video revealing their season schedule. It looks like this season, they want to finish the fight, as they have a Halo-themed package.

Each game was represented with a unique stage, such as a bunch of cookies in the Cookie Contraband stage, when previewing their week 3 game against the Buffalo Bills. Their week 7 bye was marked by a relaxing beach view, and their final game, week 18 against the Denver Broncos, saw them finish the fight.

At the end of the package, Halo Studios was thanked. As was Microsoft, Xbox, and the Halo Community, among others. Check out the full video below.

Los Angeles Chargers Full 2026 Schedule

Below is the full schedule for the Los Angeles Chargers, courtesy of their official website. You can also see where to stream the games for the ones that have already been announced.

2026 LOS ANGELES CHARGERS REGULAR-SEASON SCHEDULE

WEEK DATE OPPONENT TIME (PT) TELEVISION

1 Sun. Sept. 13 Arizona Cardinals 1:25 pm CBS

2 Sun. Sept. 20 Las Vegas Raiders 1:05 pm CBS

3 Sun. Sept. 27 at Buffalo Bills 10:00 am FOX

4 Sun. Oct. 4 at Seattle Seahawks 1:25 pm CBS

5 Sun. Oct. 11 Denver Broncos 1:05 pm CBS

6 Sun. Oct. 18 at Kansas City Chiefs 1:25 pm CBS

7 Sun. Oct. 25 BYE — —

8 Sun. Nov. 1 at Los Angeles Rams 1:05 pm FOX

9 Sun. Nov. 8 Houston Texans 1:05 pm CBS

10 Mon. Nov. 16 at Baltimore Ravens 5:15 pm ESPN

11 Sun. Nov. 22 New York Jets 1:05 pm FOX

12 Sun. Nov. 29 New England Patriots 5:20 pm NBC

13 Sun. Dec. 6 at Tampa Bay Buccaneers 10:00 am CBS

14 Sun. Dec. 13 at Las Vegas Raiders 1:05 pm CBS

15 Thurs. Dec. 17 San Francisco 49ers 5:15 pm PRIME VIDEO

16 Sun. Dec. 27 at Miami Dolphins 10:00 am FOX

17 TBD Kansas City Chiefs TBD TBD

18 TBD at Denver Broncos 

For those who want to play football video games, don’t expect the NFL 2K series to be revived anytime soon. It was recently confirmed that the planned NFL 2K comeback game was officially pulled from development. Learn more about that here.

What do you think about the schedule for the LA Chargers? How did you like their Halo-inspired video package? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Ex-EA Exec Says AI Cannot Save Poor Game Development Teams
item.content:encoded:
David Gardner, a former Electronic Arts executive and Atari CEO, is now a co-founder of London Venture Partners. He was one of the first EA employees, 11th overall, back in 1982, and he worked for that company until 2007. While he was there, he helped found EA Europe and played a huge role in the EA Sports franchise in the region.

He is now using his experience in the gaming industry to support new projects in this field. In a recent interview, he discussed how AI in gaming could attract investors back to the video game market. He also acknowledges that venture capitalists’ overall entry into the gaming sector has not been very successful.

Former EA Exec Says ‘AI and Small Teams’ Is Business Model For New IP

Speaking with The Game Business, the gaming exec turned venture capitalist explains that using AI and working with a small team are the new, feasible business models for new IP in gaming. As has been the recent talking point, AI theoretically enables greater efficiency and productivity in development.

“AI and small teams… that is the new business model, at least for a new IP. You get in, you start the flywheel moving, your community, you get your cash flow coming in because people are spending. And then you go big. You don’t have to have a mega team. You have more and more AI agents running to help your development be productive.”

Of course, he says the technology only works if the development team is actually good. Gardner stressed that they will have to weed through the bad AI projects just as they did with Web3 and VR. In the end, he believes AI will only help human developers get to where they’re going faster, even if that means a faster road to a bad project.

“Can they get the quality? Are the individuals any good? Because the AI will just get them quicker to some place, whether it’s a good place or a bad place. My work as an investor is to figure out: is that team going to be able to nail it? Are they going to be able to understand what the customers will love? And can they even get along during the journey?”

For more discussions on AI and gaming, check out how Capcom says they’re using Generative AI to scale back time spent on mundane tasks that detract from the creative side of game development. Also, check out Sony’s recent comments on AI and the future of PlayStation gaming.

Be sure to sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #802 Hints and Answers for Today, May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #802 for Thursday, May 14, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, May 14, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #802 are all related to springtime veggies you can grow in your garden.

General Strands Hints For May 14

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Dana Carvey was a master of this.
  • Hint 2: ___ Tuck.
  • Hint 3: Steal from the rich, give to the poor.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #802 for May 14, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for May 14 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • REST
  • RESTS
  • HOOD
  • ROBS
  • RIFFS

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #802 On May 14, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • FRIAR
  • FOREST
  • DISGUISE
  • OUTLAW
  • ARCHERY
  • SHERIFF
  • SPANAGRAM: ROBINHOOD

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #802?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 15 will be Strands Game #803.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
